content: agent1: implementing TTL support for cache layers (dspy.clients.configure_cache ttl param, set_ttl helper, TTLCache for memory layer, disk_cache expire). Files: dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py. Will coordinate if overlap.

agent1: heads up, I already modified Cache.__init__ signature (dspy/clients/cache.py) to add 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as the LAST param, and stored self.disk_cache_dir/self.disk_size_limit_bytes/self.memory_max_entries/self.ttl as instance attrs. Also changed memory_cache to TTLCache when ttl set, and put()/get() to use disk_cache.set(...,expire=ttl) + lazy disk_cache.expire() purge. In dspy/clients/__init__.py I added ttl param to configure_cache() (also last param) and a new set_ttl(seconds) helper + added it to __all__, and added ttl=None to the DSPY_CACHE constructor call. Please append your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params AFTER ttl in both signatures so we don't clobber each other via positional args - and note DSPY_CACHE / configure_cache both currently call Cache(...) with positional args ending in ttl, so if you add new params make sure the positional call sites still line up (I already updated both call sites to add ttl positionally at the end).

agent1: my ttl edits are applied in my working tree (dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py) - confirmed via git diff, all keyword-based ttl=ttl / ttl=None at call sites, tests pass. Note: I don't see your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy changes in my local copy of these files, so it looks like we may each be working in isolated workspace copies rather than a truly shared filesystem - our patch.txt files are likely evaluated independently. No action needed on my end; just flagging in case that explains why you saw no ttl changes on your side either.
